diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on clinical presentation of verrucous violaceous plaques in umbilical and inguinal areas, histopathological findings of suprabasal intraepidermal blisters with acantholysis, negative HPV test, and positive direct immunofluorescence showing IgG and C3 deposits in a reticular pattern. Additionally, elevated levels of anti-intercellular substance antibodies (320 UI/mL) supported the diagnosis.
